title: VS Code Linter Extension for Samantha SDK
topic: Option A — VS Code standalone extension for the Samantha SDK linter: how to build a VS Code extension that uses the TypeScript compiler API to parse .ts/.js files and validate samantha.* call sites, registers Diagnostic/Completion/Hover/Snippet providers via vscode Extension API, validates address strings like samantha._viewport.<slot>._<kind>.<name>, and publishes to the VS Code Marketplace.
summary: Samantha SDK VS Code extension: diagnostics, completions, hover, snippets, and Marketplace publishing.
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Overview
  2. 2. Key Findings
  3. 3. Extension Architecture
  4. 4. Diagnostics
  5. 5. TypeScript Compiler API
  6. 6. Address String Validation
  7. 7. Completion & Hover Providers
  8. 8. Snippets
  9. 9. Marketplace Publishing
  10. 10. Data Tables
  11. 11. Sources
  12. 12. Gaps

1. Overview

A VS Code extension for the Samantha SDK can be built entirely without a Language Server Protocol (LSP) server by using the VS Code Extension API directly (vscode.languages.*) paired with TypeScript's compiler API for fast syntax-only AST walking. The extension activates on TypeScript/JavaScript files, parses samantha.* call sites, validates ArtefactType string literals and address strings, provides completions and hover docs, and bundles declarative snippets — all in one publishable package.

2. Key Findings

  • No Language Server needed. vscode.languages.createDiagnosticCollection, registerCompletionItemProvider, and registerHoverProvider all work in-process without a separate LSP server. LSP is only needed when targeting multiple editors.
  • ts.createSourceFile without a full Program is fast. Syntax-only AST parsing (no type checker, no tsconfig load) is sufficient for all Samantha SDK validation — checking string literal values against a known union set requires no type inference.
  • Debounce onDidChangeTextDocument. Parse on every keystroke is expensive; 300–500 ms debounce is the standard pattern from Microsoft's own samples.
  • Activation events should be ["onLanguage:typescript", "onLanguage:javascript"] — not "*", which activates on every VS Code launch.
  • Marketplace publishing requires an Azure DevOps publisher account, @vscode/vsce CLI, and a PNG icon ≥128×128 px (no SVG). Verified-publisher status takes up to 5 business days.
  • FieldName validation deferred. Valid field names are only known at runtime from the schema API, not statically — deferred scope for arg 2 of getValue/setValue.

3. Extension Architecture & Scaffold

Generated via yo code (Yeoman + generator-code). Select "New Extension (TypeScript)". As of VS Code 1.74+, onLanguage:* events no longer need a paired activationEvents entry when contributed via contributes.languages — but external extensions targeting built-in language IDs still need explicit declaration.

Minimum package.json fields:

{
  "name": "samantha-sdk",
  "displayName": "Samantha SDK",
  "publisher": "mmffdev",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "engines": { "vscode": "^1.74.0" },
  "icon": "icon.png",
  "activationEvents": [
    "onLanguage:typescript",
    "onLanguage:javascript",
    "onLanguage:typescriptreact",
    "onLanguage:javascriptreact"
  ],
  "contributes": {
    "snippets": [
      { "language": "typescript", "path": "./snippets/samantha.json" },
      { "language": "typescriptreact", "path": "./snippets/samantha.json" }
    ]
  }
}

Recommended source layout:

src/
  extension.ts     // activate() / deactivate()
  diagnostics.ts   // DiagnosticCollection + document change subscriptions
  validator.ts     // AST walker, ArtefactType + address validation
  completions.ts   // CompletionItemProvider
  hover.ts         // HoverProvider
snippets/
  samantha.json

Completions and hover providers are registered programmatically in activate() — there are no package.json contribution points for them; snippets are the only declarative contribution.

4. Diagnostics — Architecture & Pattern

createDiagnosticCollection is the VS Code primitive for squiggle-and-Problems-panel diagnostics. Subscribe to three document events: onDidOpenTextDocument, onDidChangeTextDocument (debounced 300 ms), and onDidCloseTextDocument (clears the collection for that URI). Each validation run parses the document via TypeScript Compiler API, maps violations to vscode.Diagnostic objects with vscode.Range, and calls collection.set(doc.uri, diagnostics).

const collection = vscode.languages.createDiagnosticCollection('samantha-sdk');
context.subscriptions.push(collection);

vscode.workspace.onDidOpenTextDocument(validate);
vscode.workspace.onDidChangeTextDocument(e => debouncedValidate(e.document));
vscode.workspace.onDidCloseTextDocument(doc => collection.delete(doc.uri));

// Inside validate():
const range = new vscode.Range(
  new vscode.Position(line, charStart),
  new vscode.Position(line, charEnd)
);
const diag = new vscode.Diagnostic(range, message, vscode.DiagnosticSeverity.Error);
diag.code = 'SAM001';
diag.source = 'samantha-sdk';
collection.set(doc.uri, diagnostics);

5. TypeScript Compiler API — AST Walking

ts.createSourceFile (syntax-only, no Program) is fast and sufficient. Pass true for setParentNodes so node.getStart() works correctly for position reporting.

import * as ts from 'typescript';

const sourceFile = ts.createSourceFile(
  doc.fileName,
  doc.getText(),
  ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,
  true  // setParentNodes
);

const ARTEFACT_TYPES = new Set([
  'execution_user_stories', 'execution_defects',
  'execution_tasks', 'execution_test_cases', 'strategic'
]);

function walk(node: ts.Node) {
  if (ts.isCallExpression(node) && isSamanthaFieldsCall(node.expression)) {
    const firstArg = node.arguments[0];
    if (firstArg && ts.isStringLiteral(firstArg) && !ARTEFACT_TYPES.has(firstArg.text)) {
      const pos = sourceFile.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(firstArg.getStart());
      // emit diagnostic at (pos.line, pos.character)
    }
  }
  ts.forEachChild(node, walk);
}
walk(sourceFile);

isSamanthaFieldsCall walks the PropertyAccessExpression chain verifying name.text is samantha → portfolio → fields → <method>. Do not use a full Program with TypeChecker — that compiles the entire project and is too slow for on-change linting.

6. Address String Validation

Detect any StringLiteral starting with samantha._viewport. and validate each segment. The rule: system segments (kind markers) start with _; user-supplied name segments never do. Segment alternation: _kind.name._kind.name…

const SLOT_RE = /^(app|header|footer|side_bar|modal|toast)$/;
const SEG_RE  = /^[a-z0-9_]{1,64}$/;

function validateAddress(raw: string): string | null {
  const parts = raw.split('.');
  if (parts[0] !== 'samantha') return null;  // not a Samantha address
  if (parts[1] !== '_viewport') return 'Expected _viewport as second segment';
  if (!SLOT_RE.test(parts[2])) return `Unknown slot "${parts[2]}" — must be app|header|footer|side_bar|modal|toast`;
  for (let i = 3; i < parts.length; i += 2) {
    if (!parts[i]?.startsWith('_')) return `Segment "${parts[i]}" must start with _`;
    const name = parts[i + 1];
    if (!name || !SEG_RE.test(name)) return `Name "${name}" must match [a-z0-9_]{1,64}`;
  }
  return null; // valid
}

7. Completion & Hover Providers

Both registered programmatically in activate(). Completions trigger on " and ' and only activate when the cursor is inside the first argument of a samantha.portfolio.fields.* call. Hover matches any samantha.* identifier and returns the method's markdown docstring.

vscode.languages.registerCompletionItemProvider(
  [{ language: 'typescript' }, { language: 'javascript' }],
  {
    provideCompletionItems(document, position) {
      if (!isInsideSamanthaArtefactArg(document, position)) return [];
      return [...ARTEFACT_TYPES].map(t => {
        const item = new vscode.CompletionItem(t, vscode.CompletionItemKind.EnumMember);
        item.detail = 'ArtefactType';
        return item;
      });
    }
  },
  '"', "'"
);

vscode.languages.registerHoverProvider(
  [{ language: 'typescript' }, { language: 'javascript' }],
  {
    provideHover(document, position) {
      const word = document.getText(document.getWordRangeAtPosition(position));
      const doc = SAMANTHA_METHOD_DOCS[word];
      return doc ? new vscode.Hover(new vscode.MarkdownString(doc)) : null;
    }
  }
);

SAMANTHA_METHOD_DOCS is a static map extracted from samantha.ts JSDoc comments at build time and bundled as a JSON file in the extension.

8. Snippets

Declared in package.json under contributes.snippets, resolved from snippets/samantha.json. TextMate syntax: $1, $2 are tab stops; ${1:placeholder} provides default text. Linked tab stops (same number used multiple times) update together when edited.

{
  "Samantha get value": {
    "prefix": "sam-get",
    "body": [
      "const schema = await samantha.portfolio.fields.getSchema('$1');",
      "const val = await samantha.portfolio.fields.getValue('$1', artefactId, 'fieldName');",
      "const unwrapped = samantha.portfolio.fields.unwrap(val!, schema.find(f => f.field_name === 'fieldName')!);"
    ],
    "description": "Get and unwrap a single Samantha field value"
  },
  "Samantha set values": {
    "prefix": "sam-set",
    "body": [
      "await samantha.portfolio.fields.setValues('$1', artefactId, {",
      "  fieldName: value,",
      "});"
    ],
    "description": "Bulk-set Samantha field values in one transaction"
  },
  "Samantha SDK Provider": {
    "prefix": "sam-provider",
    "body": [
      "<SamanthaSdkProvider customAppId=\"app-id\">",
      "  $0",
      "</SamanthaSdkProvider>"
    ],
    "description": "Wrap a custom app frame in the Samantha SDK context provider"
  }
}

9. Marketplace Publishing

StepTool / Command
Create Azure DevOps orgmarketplace.visualstudio.com
Generate PAT (Marketplace: Manage, All orgs)Azure DevOps → User settings → PAT
Create publisher IDmarketplace.visualstudio.com/manage
Install vsce CLInpm install -g @vscode/vsce
Authenticatevsce login mmffdev
Test locallyF5 in VS Code → Extension Dev Host
Packagevsce package → produces .vsix
Publishvsce publish
Verify publisher (optional — adds verified badge)DNS TXT record + Marketplace review ≤35 days

Required package.json fields: name, version, publisher, engines.vscode, icon (PNG ≥128×128 px, no SVG). Limit keywords to 30 max. All image URLs in README must use HTTPS. Prefer macOS/Linux for the publish step — building on Windows can lose POSIX file attributes on the .vsix.

10. Data Tables

ArtefactType union (current samantha.ts):

ValueDescription
execution_user_storiesUser story artefacts
execution_defectsDefect / bug artefacts
execution_tasksTask artefacts
execution_test_casesTest case artefacts
strategicStrategic planning artefacts

samantha.portfolio.fields methods — validated arguments:

MethodArg 0Arg 1Arg 2
getSchemaArtefactType ✓——
getValuesArtefactType ✓artefactID—
getValueArtefactType ✓artefactIDfieldName (deferred)
setValueArtefactType ✓artefactIDfieldName (deferred)
setValuesArtefactType ✓artefactIDvalues (object)
unwrapFieldValueSchemaField—

Viewport slots (address string validation):

SlotUse
appMain page body (default)
headerTop bar / breadcrumb strip
footerBottom bar / status strip
side_barPersistent left/right rails
modalModal / dialog overlays
toastTransient toast / snackbar stack

12. Gaps & Limitations

  • FieldName validation deferred. Arg 2 of getValue/setValue requires knowing the active schema at runtime — cannot be validated statically without bundling a schema snapshot.
  • ts-morph not benchmarked. Adds ~2 MB to the extension bundle; raw typescript package preferred. No head-to-head parse-speed benchmark was found.
  • Debounce approach. Standard 300 ms manual setTimeout is referenced but not benchmarked vs. VS Code's built-in CancellationToken pattern.
  • JSX / TSX support. Additional onLanguage:typescriptreact / onLanguage:javascriptreact activation events needed; not explicitly benchmarked.
  • Publisher verification timeline. Up to 5 business days — plan before a launch date.
  • Windows publish risk. Building/publishing from Windows can lose POSIX file attributes on the .vsix — prefer macOS or Linux.
